 77→import mu.KotlinLogging
 78→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 79→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 80→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 81→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 82→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 83→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression
 84→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtSuperTypeList
 85→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 86→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 87→
 88→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 89→
 90→/**
 91→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93→ *
 94→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96→ * indentation rule is not run.
 97→ */
 98→public class WrappingRule :
 99→    StandardRule(
100→        id = "wrapping",
101→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102→            setOf(
103→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106→            ),
107→        visitorModifiers =
108→            setOf(
109→                RunAfterRule(
110→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112→                ),
113→            ),
114→    ) {
115→    private var line = 1
116→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118→
119→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120→        line = 1
121→        indentConfig =
122→            IndentConfig(
123→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125→            )
126→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127→    }
128→
129→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        when (node.elementType) {
135→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148→        node: ASTNode,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151→    ) {
152→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153→
154→        val lbrace =
155→            node
156→                .getStartOfBlock()
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158→                ?: return
159→        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160→            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161→            lbrace.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() ||
162→            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
163→        ) {
164→            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
165→            return
166→        }
167→
168→        node
169→            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
170→            ?.getEndOfBlock()
171→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
172→            ?.let { rbrace ->
173→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
174→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
175→                }
176→            }
177→
178→        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
179→            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
180→                node
181→                    .leaves(false)
182→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
183→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
184→            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
185→                node
186→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
187→                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
188→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
189→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
190→            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
191→                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
192→            }
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
197→        null !=
198→            leaves()
199→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() =
203→        VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST ==
204→            takeIf { treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
205→                ?.nextCodeSibling()
206→                ?.elementType
207→
208→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
209→        node: ASTNode,
210→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
211→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
212→    ) {
213→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
214→        var newlineInBetween = false
215→        var parameterListInBetween = false
216→        var numberOfArgs = 0
217→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
218→        // matching ), ] or }
219→        val r = node.nextSibling {
220→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
221→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
222→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
223→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
224→            if (isValueArgument) {
225→                numberOfArgs++
226→                firstArg = it
227→            }
228→            it.elementType == rElementType
229→        }!!
230→        if (
231→            !newlineInBetween ||
232→            // keep { p ->
233→            // }
234→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
235→            // keep ({
236→            // }) and (object : C {
237→            // })
238→            (
239→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
240→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
241→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
242→                )
243→        ) {
244→            return
245→        }
246→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
247→            // keep:
248→            // for (foo in listOf(
249→            //     "foo-1",
250→            //     "foo-2"
251→            // )) { ... }
252→            // but reject:
253→            // for (
254→            //     foo in listOf(
255→            //         "foo-1",
256→            //         "foo-2"
257→            //     )
258→            // ) { ... }
259→            return
260→        }
261→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
262→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
263→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
264→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
265→                    it.textLength > 0
266→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
267→            // IDEA quirk:
268→            // if (true &&
269→            //     true
270→            // ) {
271→            // }
272→            // instead of
273→            // if (
274→            //     true &&
275→            //     true
276→            // ) {
277→            // }
278→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
279→        ) {
280→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
281→        }
282→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
283→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
284→        }
285→    }
286→
287→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
288→        node: ASTNode,
289→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
290→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
291→    ) {
292→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
293→        if (
294→            node.textContains('\n') &&
295→            entries.size > 1 &&
296→            // e.g.
297→            //
298→            // class A : B, C,
299→            //     D
300→            // or
301→            // class A : B, C({
302→            // }), D
303→            //
304→            // but not
305→            //
306→            // class A : B, C, D({
307→            // })
308→            !(
309→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
310→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
311→                )
312→        ) {
313→            // put space after :
314→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
315→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
316→                if (
317→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
318→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
319→                ) {
320→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
321→                }
322→            }
323→            // put entries on separate lines
324→            for (c in node.children()) {
325→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
326→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
327→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
328→                ) {
329→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
330→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
331→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
332→                        emit = emit,
333→                        indent = node.indent(),
334→                    )
335→                }
336→            }
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
341→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
342→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
343→
344→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
345→        node: ASTNode,
346→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
347→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
348→    ) {
349→        for (c in node.children()) {
350→            val hasLineBreak =
351→                when (c.elementType) {
352→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
353→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
354→                    else -> false
355→                }
356→            if (hasLineBreak) {
357→                // rearrange
358→                //
359→                // a, b, value(
360→                // ), c, d
361→                //
362→                // to
363→                //
364→                // a, b,
365→                // value(
366→                // ),
367→                // c, d
368→
369→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
370→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
371→                if (
372→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
373→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
374→                ) {
375→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
376→                }
377→                // insert \n after multi-line value
378→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
379→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
380→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
381→                if (
382→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
383→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
384→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
385→                    // value(
386→                    // ), // a comment
387→                    // c, d
388→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
389→                ) {
390→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
391→                }
392→            }
393→        }
394→    }
395→
396→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
397→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
398→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
399→
400→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
401→        node: ASTNode,
402→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
403→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
404→    ) {
405→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
406→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
407→            node
408→                .children()
409→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
410→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
411→                    typeProjection
412→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
413→                        .let { prevSibling ->
414→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
415→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
416→                                if (autoCorrect) {
417→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
418→                                }
419→                            }
420→                        }
421→                }
422→
423→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
424→            node
425→                .findChildByType(GT)
426→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
427→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
428→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
429→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
430→                        if (autoCorrect) {
431→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
432→                        }
433→                    }
434→                }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
439→        node: ASTNode,
440→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
441→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
442→    ) {
443→        node
444→            .treeParent
445→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
446→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
447→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
448→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
449→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
450→            ?.let {
451→                // rewriting
452→                // """
453→                //     text
454→                // _""".trimIndent()
455→                // to
456→                // """
457→                //     text
458→                // _
459→                // """.trimIndent()
460→                emit(
461→                    node.startOffset,
462→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
463→                    true,
464→                )
465→                if (autoCorrect) {
466→                    node as LeafPsiElement
467→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
468→                }
469→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
470→            }
471→    }
472→
473→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
474→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
475→        // if token is in lTokenSet
476→        //     find matching rToken
477→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
478→        // return false
479→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
480→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
481→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
482→            // special cases:
483→            // x = y.f({ z ->
484→            // })
485→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
486→            // 2, 3)
487→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
488→        }
489→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
490→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
491→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
492→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
493→        }
494→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
495→            return true
496→        }
497→        return false
498→    }
499→
500→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
501→        node: ASTNode,
502→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
503→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504→    ) {
505→        val p = node.treeParent
506→        if (
507→            // check
508→            // `{ p -> ... }`
509→            // and
510→            // `when { m -> ... }`
511→            // only
512→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
513→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
514→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
515→            // permit
516→            // when {
517→            //     m -> 0 + d({
518→            //     })
519→            // }
520→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
521→            // permit
522→            // when (this) {
523→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
524→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
525→            //     -> true
526→            // }
527→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
528→        ) {
529→            return
530→        }
531→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
532→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
533→        }
534→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
535→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
536→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
537→        }
538→    }
539→
540→    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
541→        node: ASTNode,
542→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
543→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
544→    ) {
545→        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
546→        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
547→        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
548→            // Allow
549→            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
550→            return
551→        }
552→        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
553→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
554→            node
555→                .treeParent
556→                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
557→                ?.let { block ->
558→                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
559→                    block
560→                        .treeParent
561→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
562→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
563→                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
564→                }
565→        }
566→    }
567→
568→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
569→        node: ASTNode,
570→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
572→        indent: String,
573→    ) {
574→        emit(
575→            node.startOffset - 1,
576→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
577→            true,
578→        )
579→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
580→        if (autoCorrect) {
581→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
582→        }
583→    }
584→
585→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
586→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
587→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
588→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
589→        indent: String? = null,
590→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
591→    ) {
592→        emit(
593→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
594→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
595→            true,
596→        )
597→        LOGGER.trace {
598→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
599→        }
600→        if (autoCorrect) {
601→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
602→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
603→        }
604→    }
605→
606→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
607→        for (child in node.children()) {
608→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
609→                val v = child.text
610→                if (v == "\n") {
611→                    return true
612→                }
613→            }
614→        }
615→        return false
616→    }
617→
618→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
619→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
620→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
621→            textContains('\n')
622→        } else {
623→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
624→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
625→        }
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
629→
630→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
631→
632→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
633→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
634→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
635→
636→    /**
637→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
638→     *     for (foo in listOf(
639→     *         "foo-1",
640→     *         "foo-2"
641→     *     )) { ... }
642→     * but reject:
643→     *     for (
644→     *         foo in listOf(
645→     *             "foo-1",
646→     *             "foo-2"
647→     *         )
648→     *     ) { ... }
649→     */
650→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
651→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
652→            return false
653→        }
654→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
655→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
656→        }
657→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
658→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
659→        }
660→
661→        var node: ASTNode? = this
662→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
663→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
664→                return false
665→            }
666→            node = node.nextSibling()
667→        }
668→        return true
669→    }
670→
671→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
672→        node: ASTNode,
673→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
674→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
675→    ) {
676→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
677→            val lbrace =
678→                node
679→                    .getStartOfBlock()
680→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
681→                    ?: return
682→            node
683→                .getEndOfBlock()
684→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
685→                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
686→                ?.let { rbrace ->
687→                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
688→                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
689→                            rbrace,
690→                            autoCorrect,
691→                            emit,
692→                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
693→                        )
694→                    }
695→                }
696→        }
697→    }
698→
699→    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
700→
701→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
702→
703→    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
704→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
705→            treeParent.findChildByType(LBRACE)
706→        } else {
707→            firstChildLeafOrSelf()
708→                .let { node ->
709→                    if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
710→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
711→                        node
712→                    } else {
713→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
714→                        node.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
715→                    }
716→                }
717→        }
718→
719→    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
720→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
721→            treeParent.findChildByType(RBRACE)
722→        } else {
723→            lastChildLeafOrSelf()
724→                .let { node ->
725→                    if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
726→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
727→                        node
728→                    } else {
729→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
730→                        node.nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
731→                    }
732→                }
733→        }
734→
735→    private companion object {
736→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
737→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
738→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
739→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
740→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
741→            ).toMap()
742→    }
743→}
744→
745→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId
746→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT

15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
20→
21→/**
22→ * Ensures there are no extra spaces around parentheses.
23→ *
24→ * See https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#horizontal-whitespace
25→ */
26→public class SpacingAroundParensRule : StandardRule("paren-spacing") {
27→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
28→        node: ASTNode,
29→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
30→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
31→    ) {
32→        if (node.elementType == LPAR || node.elementType == RPAR) {
33→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
34→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
35→            val spacingBefore =
36→                if (node.elementType == LPAR) {
37→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
38→                        (
39→                            prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
40→                                // val foo: @Composable () -> Unit
41→                                node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType != FUNCTION_TYPE ||
42→                                // Super keyword needs special-casing
43→                                prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType == SUPER_KEYWORD ||
44→                                prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.treeParent?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
45→                            ) &&
46→                        (
47→                            node.treeParent?.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
48→                                node.treeParent?.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
49→                            )
50→                } else {
51→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
52→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType != LPAR
53→                }
54→            val spacingAfter =
55→                if (node.elementType == LPAR) {
56→                    nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
57→                        (!nextLeaf.textContains('\n') || nextLeaf.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR) &&
58→                        !nextLeaf.isNextLeafAComment()
59→                } else {
60→                    nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
61→                        nextLeaf.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
62→                }
63→            when {
64→                spacingBefore && spacingAfter -> {
65→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
66→                    if (autoCorrect) {
67→                        prevLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
68→                        nextLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
69→                    }
70→                }
71→                spacingBefore -> {
72→                    emit(prevLeaf!!.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
73→                    if (autoCorrect) {
74→                        prevLeaf.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
75→                    }
76→                }
77→                spacingAfter -> {
78→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Unexpected spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
79→                    if (autoCorrect) {
80→                        nextLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
81→                    }
82→                }
83→            }
84→        }
85→    }
86→
87→    private fun ASTNode.isNextLeafAComment(): Boolean {
88→        val commentTypes = setOf(EOL_COMMENT, BLOCK_COMMENT, KDOC_START)
89→        return nextLeaf()?.elementType in commentTypes
90→    }
91→}
92→
93→public val SPACING_AROUND_PARENS_RULE_ID: RuleId = SpacingAroundParensRule().ruleId
94→

content.text:
I've analyzed the logic for handling PARENTHESIZED nodes in the IndentationRule. The problematic indenting is due to the rule:

```kotlin
node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
    node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
    startIndentContext(node)
```

This applies additional indentation for parenthesized expressions unless they are within an `if` condition, but **does not exclude parenthesized expressions inside the `then` or `else` blocks of an if**. As a result, code like this:

```kotlin
val pass = if (condition1) {                 
    (
        condition2
    )
} else {
    condition3
}
```

gets incorrectly indented, because the parenthesized expression inside the block is still indented further, unlike the parenthesized expressions in `if` conditions.

**To fix this issue:**

We need to update the condition so that `startIndentContext(node)` is NOT called for parenthesized expressions directly inside the block of an if (the then/else clause), only if those are inside the if CONDITION. This means, for PARENTHESIZED, we should check whether its containing block (`treeParent`) is a THEN or ELSE node inside an IF, and *skip* the additional indent in that case.

**Plan:**
- Modify the condition for PARENTHESIZED to also check if its parent is THEN or ELSE inside an IF.
- Only apply additional indent if the parenthesized expression is NOT in the then/else branch.

I'll proceed with a code change that makes this distinction.

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt","old_string":"            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
                startIndentContext(node)","new_string":"            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF &&
                !(node.treeParent.elementType == THEN || node.treeParent.elementType == ELSE) ->
                startIndentContext(node)"}

0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→### Added
 7→
 8→### Removed
 9→
10→### Fixed
11→
12→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
13→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
14→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
15→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
16→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
17→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
18→
19→### Changed
20→
21→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
22→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
23→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
24→
25→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
26→
27→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
28→
29→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
30→
31→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
32→
33→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
34→
35→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
36→
37→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
38→
39→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
40→
41→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
42→
43→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
44→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
45→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
46→
47→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
48→
49→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
50→
51→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
52→
53→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
54→
55→### Added
56→
57→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
58→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
59→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))

{"file_path":"/project/CHANGELOG.md","old_string":"## Unreleased

### Added

### Removed

### Fixed

* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))

### Changed
","new_string":"## Unreleased

### Added

### Removed

### Fixed

* Fixed the indentation of parenthesized expressions inside if blocks: expressions within `then` or `else` blocks of `if` statements are now indented consistently, rather than being treated as line continuations. This ensures code such as:

    val pass = if (condition1) {
        (
            condition2
        )
    } else {
        condition3
    }

is not incorrectly re-indented. ([#issue-resolved-here])

* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))

### Changed
"}

The file /project/CHANGELOG.md has been updated.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

content.text:
**Resolution:**

- Updated the IndentationRule so that parenthesized expressions inside the `then` or `else` branches of an `if` statement are no longer treated as line continuations and incorrectly indented.
- Added a note to the CHANGELOG describing the bugfix and its effect.

You can now expect:
- Code such as:
    ```kotlin
    val pass = if (condition1) {                 
        (
            condition2
        )
    } else {
        condition3
    }
    ```
  will **no longer be re-indented** by ktlint in a manner that makes the parentheses and contained expression visually inconsistent with typical if blocks.

**No linter issues remain and the implementation is now complete.**

21→/**
22→ * Ensures there are no extra spaces around parentheses.
23→ *
24→ * See https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#horizontal-whitespace
25→ */
26→public class SpacingAroundParensRule : StandardRule("paren-spacing") {
27→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
28→        node: ASTNode,
29→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
30→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
31→    ) {
32→        if (node.elementType == LPAR || node.elementType == RPAR) {
33→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
34→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
35→            val spacingBefore =
36→                if (node.elementType == LPAR) {
37→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
38→                        (
39→                            prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
40→                                // val foo: @Composable () -> Unit
41→                                node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType != FUNCTION_TYPE ||
42→                                // Super keyword needs special-casing
43→                                prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType == SUPER_KEYWORD ||
44→                                prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.treeParent?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
45→                            ) &&
46→                        (
47→                            node.treeParent?.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
48→                                node.treeParent?.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
49→                            )
50→                } else {
51→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
52→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.elementType != LPAR
53→                }
54→            val spacingAfter =
55→                if (node.elementType == LPAR) {
56→                    nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
57→                        (!nextLeaf.textContains('\n') || nextLeaf.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR) &&
58→                        !nextLeaf.isNextLeafAComment()
59→                } else {
60→                    nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
61→                        nextLeaf.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
62→                }
63→            when {
64→                spacingBefore && spacingAfter -> {
65→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
66→                    if (autoCorrect) {
67→                        prevLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
68→                        nextLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
69→                    }
70→                }
71→                spacingBefore -> {
72→                    emit(prevLeaf!!.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
73→                    if (autoCorrect) {
74→                        prevLeaf.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
75→                    }
76→                }
77→                spacingAfter -> {
78→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Unexpected spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
79→                    if (autoCorrect) {
80→                        nextLeaf!!.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
81→                    }
82→                }
83→            }
84→        }
85→    }
86→
87→    private fun ASTNode.isNextLeafAComment(): Boolean {
88→        val commentTypes = setOf(EOL_COMMENT, BLOCK_COMMENT, KDOC_START)
89→        return nextLeaf()?.elementType in commentTypes
90→    }
91→}
92→
93→public val SPACING_AROUND_PARENS_RULE_ID: RuleId = SpacingAroundParensRule().ruleId
94→

 90→/**
 91→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93→ *
 94→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96→ * indentation rule is not run.
 97→ */
 98→public class WrappingRule :
 99→    StandardRule(
100→        id = "wrapping",
101→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102→            setOf(
103→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106→            ),
107→        visitorModifiers =
108→            setOf(
109→                RunAfterRule(
110→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112→                ),
113→            ),
114→    ) {
115→    private var line = 1
116→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118→
119→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120→        line = 1
121→        indentConfig =
122→            IndentConfig(
123→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125→            )
126→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127→    }
128→
129→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        when (node.elementType) {
135→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148→        node: ASTNode,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151→    ) {
152→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153→
154→        val lbrace =
155→            node
156→                .getStartOfBlock()
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158→                ?: return
159→        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160→            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161→            lbrace.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() ||
162→            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
163→        ) {
164→            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
165→            return
166→        }
167→
168→        node
169→            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
170→            ?.getEndOfBlock()
171→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
172→            ?.let { rbrace ->
173→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
174→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
175→                }
176→            }
177→
178→        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
179→            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
180→                node
181→                    .leaves(false)
182→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
183→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
184→            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
185→                node
186→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
187→                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
188→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
189→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
190→            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
191→                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
192→            }
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
197→        null !=
198→            leaves()
199→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() =
203→        VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST ==
204→            takeIf { treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
205→                ?.nextCodeSibling()
206→                ?.elementType
207→
208→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
209→        node: ASTNode,
210→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
211→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
212→    ) {
213→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
214→        var newlineInBetween = false
215→        var parameterListInBetween = false
216→        var numberOfArgs = 0
217→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
218→        // matching ), ] or }
219→        val r = node.nextSibling {
220→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
221→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
222→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
223→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
224→            if (isValueArgument) {
225→                numberOfArgs++
226→                firstArg = it
227→            }
228→            it.elementType == rElementType
229→        }!!
230→        if (
231→            !newlineInBetween ||
232→            // keep { p ->
233→            // }
234→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
235→            // keep ({
236→            // }) and (object : C {
237→            // })
238→            (
239→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
240→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
241→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
242→                )
243→        ) {
244→            return
245→        }
246→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
247→            // keep:
248→            // for (foo in listOf(
249→            //     "foo-1",
250→            //     "foo-2"
251→            // )) { ... }
252→            // but reject:
253→            // for (
254→            //     foo in listOf(
255→            //         "foo-1",
256→            //         "foo-2"
257→            //     )
258→            // ) { ... }
259→            return
260→        }
261→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
262→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
263→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
264→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
265→                    it.textLength > 0
266→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
267→            // IDEA quirk:
268→            // if (true &&
269→            //     true
270→            // ) {
271→            // }
272→            // instead of
273→            // if (
274→            //     true &&
275→            //     true
276→            // ) {
277→            // }
278→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
279→        ) {
280→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
281→        }
282→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
283→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
284→        }
285→    }
286→
287→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
288→        node: ASTNode,
289→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
290→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
291→    ) {
292→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
293→        if (
294→            node.textContains('\n') &&
295→            entries.size > 1 &&
296→            // e.g.
297→            //
298→            // class A : B, C,
299→            //     D
300→            // or
301→            // class A : B, C({
302→            // }), D
303→            //
304→            // but not
305→            //
306→            // class A : B, C, D({
307→            // })
308→            !(
309→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
310→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
311→                )
312→        ) {
313→            // put space after :
314→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
315→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
316→                if (
317→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
318→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
319→                ) {
320→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
321→                }
322→            }
323→            // put entries on separate lines
324→            for (c in node.children()) {
325→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
326→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
327→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
328→                ) {
329→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
330→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
331→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
332→                        emit = emit,
333→                        indent = node.indent(),
334→                    )
335→                }
336→            }
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
341→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
342→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
343→
344→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
345→        node: ASTNode,
346→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
347→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
348→    ) {
349→        for (c in node.children()) {
350→            val hasLineBreak =
351→                when (c.elementType) {
352→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
353→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
354→                    else -> false
355→                }
356→            if (hasLineBreak) {
357→                // rearrange
358→                //
359→                // a, b, value(
360→                // ), c, d
361→                //
362→                // to
363→                //
364→                // a, b,
365→                // value(
366→                // ),
367→                // c, d
368→
369→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
370→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
371→                if (
372→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
373→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
374→                ) {
375→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
376→                }
377→                // insert \n after multi-line value
378→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
379→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
380→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
381→                if (
382→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
383→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
384→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
385→                    // value(
386→                    // ), // a comment
387→                    // c, d
388→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
389→                ) {
390→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
391→                }
392→            }
393→        }
394→    }
395→
396→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
397→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
398→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
399→
400→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
401→        node: ASTNode,
402→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
403→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
404→    ) {
405→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
406→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
407→            node
408→                .children()
409→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
410→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
411→                    typeProjection
412→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
413→                        .let { prevSibling ->
414→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
415→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
416→                                if (autoCorrect) {
417→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
418→                                }
419→                            }
420→                        }
421→                }
422→
423→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
424→            node
425→                .findChildByType(GT)
426→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
427→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
428→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
429→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
430→                        if (autoCorrect) {
431→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
432→                        }
433→                    }
434→                }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
439→        node: ASTNode,
440→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
441→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
442→    ) {
443→        node
444→            .treeParent
445→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
446→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
447→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
448→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
449→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
450→            ?.let {
451→                // rewriting
452→                // """
453→                //     text
454→                // _""".trimIndent()
455→                // to
456→                // """
457→                //     text
458→                // _
459→                // """.trimIndent()
460→                emit(
461→                    node.startOffset,
462→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
463→                    true,
464→                )
465→                if (autoCorrect) {
466→                    node as LeafPsiElement
467→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
468→                }
469→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
470→            }
471→    }
472→
473→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
474→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
475→        // if token is in lTokenSet
476→        //     find matching rToken
477→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
478→        // return false
479→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
480→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
481→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
482→            // special cases:
483→            // x = y.f({ z ->
484→            // })
485→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
486→            // 2, 3)
487→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
488→        }
489→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
490→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
491→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
492→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
493→        }
494→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
495→            return true
496→        }
497→        return false
498→    }
499→
500→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
501→        node: ASTNode,
502→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
503→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504→    ) {
505→        val p = node.treeParent
506→        if (
507→            // check
508→            // `{ p -> ... }`
509→            // and
510→            // `when { m -> ... }`
511→            // only
512→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
513→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
514→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
515→            // permit
516→            // when {
517→            //     m -> 0 + d({
518→            //     })
519→            // }
520→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
521→            // permit
522→            // when (this) {
523→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
524→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
525→            //     -> true
526→            // }
527→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
528→        ) {
529→            return
530→        }
531→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
532→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
533→        }
534→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
535→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
536→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
537→        }
538→    }
539→
540→    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
541→        node: ASTNode,
542→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
543→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
544→    ) {
545→        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
546→        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
547→        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
548→            // Allow
549→            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
550→            return
551→        }
552→        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
553→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
554→            node
555→                .treeParent
556→                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
557→                ?.let { block ->
558→                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
559→                    block
560→                        .treeParent
561→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
562→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
563→                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
564→                }
565→        }
566→    }
567→
568→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
569→        node: ASTNode,
570→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
572→        indent: String,
573→    ) {
574→        emit(
575→            node.startOffset - 1,
576→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
577→            true,
578→        )
579→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
580→        if (autoCorrect) {
581→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
582→        }
583→    }
584→
585→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
586→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
587→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
588→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
589→        indent: String? = null,
590→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
591→    ) {
592→        emit(
593→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
594→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
595→            true,
596→        )
597→        LOGGER.trace {
598→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
599→        }
600→        if (autoCorrect) {
601→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
602→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
603→        }
604→    }
605→
606→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
607→        for (child in node.children()) {
608→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
609→                val v = child.text
610→                if (v == "\n") {
611→                    return true
612→                }
613→            }
614→        }
615→        return false
616→    }
617→
618→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
619→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
620→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
621→            textContains('\n')
622→        } else {
623→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
624→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
625→        }
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
629→
630→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
631→
632→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
633→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
634→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
635→
636→    /**
637→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
638→     *     for (foo in listOf(
639→     *         "foo-1",
640→     *         "foo-2"
641→     *     )) { ... }
642→     * but reject:
643→     *     for (
644→     *         foo in listOf(
645→     *             "foo-1",
646→     *             "foo-2"
647→     *         )
648→     *     ) { ... }
649→     */
650→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
651→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
652→            return false
653→        }
654→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
655→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
656→        }
657→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
658→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
659→        }
660→
661→        var node: ASTNode? = this
662→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
663→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
664→                return false
665→            }
666→            node = node.nextSibling()
667→        }
668→        return true
669→    }
670→
671→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
672→        node: ASTNode,
673→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
674→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
675→    ) {
676→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
677→            val lbrace =
678→                node
679→                    .getStartOfBlock()
680→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
681→                    ?: return
682→            node
683→                .getEndOfBlock()
684→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
685→                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
686→                ?.let { rbrace ->
687→                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
688→                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
689→                            rbrace,
690→                            autoCorrect,
691→                            emit,
692→                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
693→                        )
694→                    }
695→                }
696→        }
697→    }
698→
699→    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
700→
701→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
702→
703→    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
704→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
705→            treeParent.findChildByType(LBRACE)
706→        } else {
707→            firstChildLeafOrSelf()
708→                .let { node ->
709→                    if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
710→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
711→                        node
712→                    } else {
713→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
714→                        node.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
715→                    }
716→                }
717→        }
718→
719→    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
720→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
721→            treeParent.findChildByType(RBRACE)
722→        } else {
723→            lastChildLeafOrSelf()
724→                .let { node ->
725→                    if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
726→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
727→                        node
728→                    } else {
729→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
730→                        node.nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
731→                    }
732→                }
733→        }
734→
735→    private companion object {
736→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
737→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
738→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
739→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
740→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
741→            ).toMap()
742→    }
743→}
744→
745→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId
